workflow_guide:
WHEN TO USE: Unsure which tool to use OR need workflow guidance OR first time using TestForge | WHAT IT DOES: Returns workflow steps. | HOW IT WORKS: Returns step-by-step sequences.
Anti-Usage: Do not use if you already know what to do.
TRIGGER: Unsure which tool to use
RETURNS: { workflows: [...] }
NEXT: Follow returned workflow steps sequentially
COST: Low
check_playwright_ready:
WHEN TO USE: Before running tests | WHAT IT DOES: Checks if Playwright, configured browsers, baseUrl, and mcp-config are valid/reachable. | HOW IT WORKS: Validates environment setup.
Anti-Usage: Do not repeat if already ready.
TRIGGER: tests won't start / check setup
RETURNS: Readiness report
NEXT: setup_project or run_playwright_test
COST: Low
scan_structural_brain:
WHEN TO USE: To identify God Nodes in the architecture before planning refactors. | WHAT IT DOES: Scans import graph and caches God Nodes. | HOW IT WORKS: Returns a list of risky files that should be modified cautiously.
Anti-Usage: Do not use for small bug fixes.
TRIGGER: Planning large refactor
RETURNS: List of God Nodes
NEXT: analyze_codebase
COST: Low
get_token_budget:
WHEN TO USE: User asks token budget or cost check | WHAT IT DOES: Returns Per-tool breakdown of estimated session token usage. | HOW IT WORKS: Reads in-memory tracking.
Anti-Usage: Do not poll continuously.
TRIGGER: User asks 'how many tokens used'
RETURNS: Budget report string
NEXT: Continue execution
COST: Low
get_system_state:
WHEN TO USE: Require previous context | WHAT IT DOES: Returns DOM scan history summary, session token cost, and turn counter. | HOW IT WORKS: Reads contextual memory.
Anti-Usage: Do not request if memory is sufficiently loaded.
TRIGGER: User asks 'what is the current state'
RETURNS: System snapshot string
NEXT: generate_gherkin_pom_test_suite
COST: Low
analyze_codebase:
WHEN TO USE: To scan existing codebase structure before generating code. | WHAT IT DOES: Analyzes the codebase using AST. | HOW IT WORKS: Parse TypeScript and Gherkin files.
Anti-Usage: Only use this for very small projects (< 5 files). FOR LARGE PROJECTS, ALWAYS USE 'execute_sandbox_code' (Turbo Mode) instead.
TRIGGER: Need project analysis
RETURNS: Analysis JSON
NEXT: generate_gherkin_pom_test_suite
COST: Medium
generate_gherkin_pom_test_suite:
WHEN TO USE: To generate a standard Playwright BDD test suite. | WHAT IT DOES: Generates feature files and POM instructions. | HOW IT WORKS: Returns a rigid system instruction context to the client LLM, ensuring the chat completion generates the requested Playwright-BDD JSON structure.
Anti-Usage: Do not generate individual methods with this.
TRIGGER: User requests test creation
RETURNS: Generation prompt
NEXT: validate_and_write
COST: High
run_playwright_test:
WHEN TO USE: AFTER generating or updating tests to verify they pass. | WHAT IT DOES: Executes the Playwright-BDD test suite natively. | HOW IT WORKS: Runs npm test or the specified command and returns the terminal output.
Anti-Usage: Do not use until validate_and_write is complete.
TRIGGER: User says 'run tests'
RETURNS: Test results / Failure DNA
NEXT: self_heal_test or summarize_suite
COST: High
upgrade_project:
WHEN TO USE: To migrate or maintain older setups. | WHAT IT DOES: Upgrades an existing Playwright-BDD project. | HOW IT WORKS: Safe and additive idempotent operation.
Anti-Usage: Do not run on non-Playwright BDD projects.
TRIGGER: User says 'update dependencies'
RETURNS: Upgrade confirmation text
NEXT: check_playwright_ready
COST: Low
inspect_page_dom:
WHEN TO USE: BEFORE generating Page Objects. | WHAT IT DOES: Navigates to a target URL in a headless browser and returns the Accessibility Tree. | HOW IT WORKS: Extracts exact locators (names, roles, test ids) to ensure 100% accuracy.
Anti-Usage: Do not use if DOM is already known.
TRIGGER: Need to see current screen
RETURNS: Semantic DOM snapshot
NEXT: generate_gherkin_pom_test_suite
COST: High
self_heal_test:
WHEN TO USE: After a run_playwright_test fails. | WHAT IT DOES: Analyzes Playwright Error DNA to determine if it's a SCRIPTING issue or an APPLICATION issue. | HOW IT WORKS: Returns a targeted heal instruction telling the AI exactly which locator to fix and how to re-inspect the live DOM.
Anti-Usage: Do not use for Syntax Errors.
TRIGGER: Test failure with 'element not found'
RETURNS: Heal instruction string
NEXT: inspect_page_dom or validate_and_write
COST: Low
validate_and_write:
WHEN TO USE: After generating code content in memory. | WHAT IT DOES: Writes the AI-generated test files to disk. | HOW IT WORKS: Performs zero-trust validation before writing.
Anti-Usage: Do not write broken code manually.
TRIGGER: LLM generated test files
RETURNS: Write confirmation string
NEXT: run_playwright_test
COST: Medium
